steps.observed: Sign-in form displayed with email, password fields and Sign in button

steps.expected: Settings page with API Keys section
steps.observed: Settings page loaded with API Keys section showing existing keys

steps.expected: API Keys section visible with Create Key button
steps.observed: API Keys section visible with 'Test UI Key' (already revoked from previous round) and '+ Create Key' button
Dialog opened with Name field, scope checkboxes, Cancel and Create Key buttons

steps.action: Fill name 'Revoke Test Key R2' and check 'Health Data (Read)' scope
steps.observed: Create Key button became enabled after filling name and selecting scope

steps.action: Click 'Create Key' to create the key
steps.expected: Key created, full key shown with Copy and I've saved it buttons
steps.observed: Key created successfully. Full key shown in yellow 'Save your API key now' panel with Copy and I've saved it buttons

steps.action: Click 'I've saved it' to dismiss creation dialog
steps.expected: Dialog closes, key list shows new active key with Revoke action
steps.observed: Dialog closed. Key list shows 'Revoke Test Key R2' as active with 'Revoke' link on the right

Confirmation dialog appeared with title 'Revoke API Key', warning message 'Are you sure you want to revoke Revoke Test Key R2? Any applications using this key will immediately lose access. This action cannot be undone.', Cancel and Revoke Key buttons

steps.expected: Key status changes to revoked with visual 'Revoked' badge
steps.observed: Dialog closed, toast notification appeared. Key now shows red 'Revoked' badge next to key name. Revoke link is no longer present.

steps.action: Verify 'Revoked' badge on the revoked key
steps.expected: Red 'Revoked' badge visible next to key name
steps.observed: Red 'Revoked' badge clearly visible next to 'Revoke Test Key R2' key name. Both keys in the list now show 'Revoked' badges.

screenshots: /tmp/ut-key-created.png (after creating new key, showing active key + save panel)
/tmp/ut-after-revoke-badge.png (CRITICAL: shows red Revoked badge on newly revoked key)

frictions.description: Create Key button in the dialog was outside the viewport due to the long list of scope checkboxes. Standard click and find commands timed out. Required using JavaScript eval to click the button directly.
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser eval to execute document.querySelector('[data-testid="create-key-submit"]').click() directly

summary: Tested 1 assertion: VAL-MPUI-014 PASSED. After revoking an API key, the UI correctly displays a red 'Revoked' badge next to the key name. The fix from the previous round (using apiKey.status === 'revoked' instead of !!apiKey.revoked_at) is confirmed working. The full flow works: clicking Revoke shows a confirmation dialog, and after confirming, the key immediately shows the Revoked badge without needing a page refresh.
